institutionName: North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University
collegeName: College of Agriculture and Environmental Sciences (CAES)
programName: Bachelor of Science in Food & Nutritional Sciences — Food Science
tagline: Science Behind the Food — From Farm to Fork
programDescription: The Food Science concentration applies chemistry, microbiology, and engineering to ensure food safety, quality, and innovation. Students work in state-of-the-art laboratories conducting research on food processing, preservation, and product development. The program prepares graduates for careers in the food industry, government regulatory agencies, and graduate research programs.
validationLabel: IFT Certification Readiness + Food Safety Competency
accreditationBody: Institute of Food Technologists (IFT) — approved program

topEmployers: NC Department of Agriculture Food Labs
careerOutlook.outlook: Food scientists are essential to ensuring the safety and quality of the global food supply. The $1.8 trillion U.S. food industry consistently needs graduates who can innovate in processing, packaging, and food safety compliance.
